title: tilia ledebourii
intro: Tilia ledebourii, also known as the Manchurian lime tree or Korean linden, is a species of deciduous tree native to East Asia, belonging to the Tilia genus of flowering plants. Traditionally, the leaves and flowers of Tilia ledebourii have been used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral effects. The health benefits associated with Tilia ledebourii include reducing anxiety and stress, improving sleep quality, and soothing digestive issues, while its herbal preparations include infusions, teas, and tinctures made from the leaves and flowers. In addition, the plant's extracts have been studied for their potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health.
